columns: MUTAGENE A.1 Tobacco or aflatoxin
MUTAGENE A.2 AID/APOBEC
MUTAGENE A.3 Unknown etiology
MUTAGENE A.4 UV radiation
MUTAGENE A.5 Deamination of methyl-cytosine
MUTAGENE B.1 Deamination of methyl-cytosine
MUTAGENE B.2 Unknown etiology
MUTAGENE B.3 Aristolochic acid
MUTAGENE B.4 Tobacco or aflatoxin
MUTAGENE B.5 AID/APOBEC
MUTAGENE B.6 Unknown etiology
MUTAGENE B.7 Unknown etiology
MUTAGENE B.8 DNA polymerase epsilon
MUTAGENE B.9 UV radiation
MUTAGENE B.10 Defective DNA mismatch repair

COSMIC 7 Ultraviolet light exposure. A large numbers of CC>TT dinucleotide mutations at dipyrimidines. A strong transcriptional strand-bias indicating that mutations occuring at pyrimidines are being repaired by transcription-coupled nucleotide excision

COSMIC 11 Mutational pattern resembling that of alkylating agents, e.g. temozolomide. Strong transcriptional strand-bias for C>T substitutions indicating transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair.
COSMIC 9 Polymerase η, which is implicated with the activity of AID during somatic hypermutation
COSMIC 10 Altered activity of the error-prone polymerase POLE. Strand bias for C>A mutations at TpCpT context and T>G mutations at TpTpT context. Ultra-hypermutator

COSMIC 3 Failure of DNA double-strand break-repair by homologous recombination. Associates strongly with elevated numbers of insertions and deletions longer than 3bp with overlapping microhomology at breakpoint junctions
COSMIC 12 Unknown etiology. A strong transcriptional strand-bias for T>C substitutions
COSMIC 15 Associated with defective DNA mismatch repair. High numbers of small (shorter than 3bp) insertions and deletions at mono/polynucleotide repeats
COSMIC 1 Endogenous mutational process initiated by spontaneous deamination of 5-methylcytosine. Correlates with age of cancer diagnosis
COSMIC 18 Unknown etiology
COSMIC 29 Associated with tobacco chewing. Transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair, associated with CC>AA dinucleotide substitutions
COSMIC 30 Unknown etiology
COSMIC 23 Unknown etiology. A very strong transcriptional strand bias for C>T mutations
COSMIC 19 Unknown etiology
COSMIC 28 Unknown etiology
COSMIC 6 Defective DNA mismatch repair and is found in microsatellite unstable tumours. High numbers of small (shorter than 3bp) insertions and deletions at mono/polynucleotide repeats

COSMIC 4 Associated with smoking and its profile is similar to the mutational pattern observed in experimental systems exposed to tobacco carcinogens (e.g., benzo[a]pyrene). Transcriptional strand bias for C>A mutations; associated with CC>AA dinucleotide substitutions
COSMIC 5 Unknown etiology. Transcriptional strand bias for T>C substitutions at ApTpN context
COSMIC 20 Associated with defective DNA mismatch repair. High numbers of small (shorter than 3bp) insertions and deletions at mono/polynucleotide repeats
COSMIC 8 Unknown etiology. A weak strand bias for C>A substitutions and is associated with double nucleotide substitutions, notably CC>AA
COSMIC 21 Unknown etiology
COSMIC 2 Activity of the AID/APOBEC family of cytidine deaminases, most likely APOBEC1, APOBEC3A and/or APOBEC3B. Transcriptional strand bias of mutations in exons
COSMIC 25 Unknown etiology. Transcriptional strand bias for T>A mutations
COSMIC 22 Exposure to aristolochic acid. Strong transcriptional strand-bias for T>A substitutions indicating transcription-coupled nucleotide excision repair
COSMIC 13 Attributed to activity of the AID/APOBEC family of cytidine deaminases, most likely APOBEC1, APOBEC3A and/or APOBEC3B
COSMIC 16 Unknown etiology. Extremely strong transcriptional strand bias for T>C mutations at ApTpN context, with T>C mutations occurring almost exclusively on the transcribed strand
COSMIC 24 Exposure to aflatoxin
COSMIC 26 Associated with defective DNA mismatch repair. A very high numbers of small (shorter than 3bp) insertions and deletions at mono/polynucleotide repeats

COSMIC 27 Unknown etiology. A very strong transcriptional strand bias for T>A mutations. Signature 27 is associated with high numbers of small (shorter than 3bp) insertions and deletions at mono/polynucleotide repeats
COSMIC 17 Unknown etiology
COSMIC 14 Unknown etiology. Ultra-hypermutator, generates very high numbers of somatic mutations
